repo_prior_art.cite: none; the API catalogue's stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap has policy-regret, not optimal-value interval honesty or length.

published_prior_art.cite: WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, arXiv:2507.11780v2, Sections 2–3: closely related constant-fit smoothed-score optimal-value inference, but not this fixed-union uniform-honesty claim; ShiLuSong2020 gives nominal-coverage optimal-value intervals.

CaiLow2004, p. 1808, Proposition 1 and Section 2.1: the two-point expected-length lower-bound mechanism for an interval honest over a union is already established; substituting a causal functional and Hellinger testing pair is routine.

ShiLuSong2020, p. 6, Conditions (A5)–(A6), and WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, Sections 2–3: margin/smoothing/nuisance balancing already provides the close optimal-value regime template; the stated split is not yet a new frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry gives an ordered expected-length frontier for an optimal-policy value.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004, Sections 2.1–2.2 and 5: ordered-modulus expected-length adaptation over finite unions, albeit for Gaussian linear functionals.

novelty_flags.label: thm:testing-modulus-lower-bound
novelty_flags.one_line: This is a direct two-point honest-interval expected-length lemma already supplied by Cai--Low's Proposition 1; the causal functional and Hellinger packaging do not create a new result.

novelty_flags.label: proposal kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=letter below novelty_target=subfield: the open upper construction and generic ordered-modulus question supply neither a matched causal adaptation frontier, a strict source-anchored improvement over a named estimator, nor a first nontrivial bound for an established method.

soundness_flags.label: ass:interior-means
soundness_flags.one_line: The asserted \(\|m_{P,a}\|_\infty\le B/2\) interior restriction is stronger than bounded outcomes and is not the standard “bounded regression envelope” supported by Audibert--Tsybakov; retag and justify it, or weaken it to the condition actually needed for the local submodel.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Proposition 1 gives a two-point lower bound on expected confidence-interval length under coverage at two parameter values, and Section 2.1 presents it as the general lower-bound basis for adaptation over a union.

The cited class uses a Tsybakov margin condition, Hölder regression membership, and a strong density assumption; it does not impose a uniform interior envelope corresponding to \(\|m_a\|_\infty\le B/2\).
Studies optimal-value inference, proves a pathwise-differentiability characterization, and develops root-n interval methodology including nonregular laws.

source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 1, abstract; pp. 5–6, Section 2.3 and Conditions (A3)–(A6)
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Develops subagging confidence intervals with nominal coverage for the optimal value, compares interval length, and uses positivity, margin, and contrast-rate conditions.
Develops adaptive smoothing for the OTR value and proves a lower asymptotic-variance bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class that its procedure attains.
Constructs softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value inference with a constant number of nuisance fits and states efficiency when treatment nonresponse has zero probability.
Proposes smoothed robust optimal-treatment-regime inference and asymptotically accurate resampling inference for both the regime parameter and optimal value function.

source_verification_receipts.version: Quantitative Economics 11 (2020), pp. 1–39, version of record
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Constructs honest bias-aware confidence intervals under specified nonparametric function-class restrictions and studies their implementation and efficiency tradeoffs.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the generic upper question with a sharp causal adaptation theorem: give explicit ordered stratum pairs with a nonzero testing-modulus penalty and a connected interval attaining the resulting rates up to constants or logarithms.

Make a theorem-level, source-located comparison with WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025: exhibit a strictly wider tie/margin/nuisance class or a strictly better uniform-honesty guarantee than its stated conditions.
Retag the \(B/2\) interior-envelope condition as novel and explain its scientific applicability, or remove it from the global class and require only the local slack needed for the testing submodel.
Demote the Cai--Low two-point theorem to supporting machinery and make the causal least-favorable modulus calculation or a matched achievable-versus-impossible adaptation boundary the focal result.

recommended_next_step: Revise around a concrete, sharp causal adaptation frontier and repair the nonstandard interior-envelope tag; otherwise pivot away from the routine finite-union uniformization kernel.
